title: rubus × pseudamoenus
intro: Rubus × pseudamoenus, also known as the black raspberry or thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the rose family and is commonly found in North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Rubus × pseudamoenus include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit anti-cancer properties due to its high content of anthocyanins and ellagic acid. Herbal preparations of Rubus × pseudamoenus can be consumed as jams, jellies, and teas, and can also be used as a topical treatment for skin irritations and wounds.
